title: Toss & Transform Object (green-screen + scale keyframes)
result_description: Toss an object toward camera and it transforms into a spinning version that flies at you, then lands back in your hand.

inputs.name: toss_clip
inputs.what_to_film: Toss the object toward camera, then film the same toss empty-handed (two takes, locked-off).

inputs.name: catch_clip
inputs.what_to_film: Film an empty catch, then a catch with the object back in your hands (two takes).

inputs.name: object_green
inputs.what_to_film: The object spinning on a green background.

edit_steps.note: split with object, split the empty version at the same point, delete the middle → object leaves the hand
split empty, split again with object in hand, delete middle → object reappears on catch

result.description: Toss an object toward camera and it transforms into a spinning version that flies at you, then lands back in your hand.
success_criteria: Object scales smoothly toward camera and back
Object vanishes/reappears cleanly at toss & catch

narration_transcript: Here's how to make this. Record yourself tossing an object towards the camera, set aside, and repeat empty-handed. Repeat with an empty catch, then again with the object back in your hands. Next, record the object spinning on a green background. In CapCut, make a split right as you toss the object, set aside, and split at the same point without it, then delete the middle. Move to the empty catch, split, then split again with the object in hand, deleting the middle. Tap overlay on your green screen clip, and drag to the left. If the frame is needed, then tap Remove Background, chroma key. Move the icon into the green area, and adjust the intensity until the green is fully removed. Tap Mask, choose Rectangle, and move to cover your object. Scroll to the beginning, reposition the object into your hand, and add a keyframe. Move to the middle, and enlarge the object towards the camera. Then go to the end, resize it back into your hands. And that's it! Follow for more tips, tap Export, and it should look like this.
